site.name: Govardhan Reddy Narala
site.headline: Data scientist and software engineer building applied ML, NLP, and scalable data systems on large datasets.
site.heroIntro: I turn complex data into reliable analytics, machine learning, and production-ready systems.
heroSummary: Data Scientist with about 3 years of experience building analytics-ready datasets, machine learning workflows, and data-intensive backend systems across enterprise and large-scale environments.
This portfolio is organized around proof: measurable production impact, large-scale applied ML projects, and systems work that moves from messy inputs to dependable delivery.
heroProofPoints: Built systems supporting 10M+ daily transactions with 99.9% reliability.
Reduced SQL query latency by 45% across data-intensive services.
Shipped ML and analytics projects on 3.6M+ records and 40K+ medical images.
Combined backend engineering depth with analytics, ML, and observability.

homeValueCards.title: Data To Delivery
homeValueCards.copy: I work well across the full path from ingestion, validation, and feature engineering to modeling, dashboards, and operational output.

homeValueCards.title: Production Context
homeValueCards.copy: My engineering background means I do not treat analytics in isolation. I think about reliability, system constraints, latency, and maintainability from the start.

homeValueCards.title: Research With Practical Value
homeValueCards.copy: My graduate projects are not toy experiments. They are framed around robustness, scale, validation, and explainable impact.

aboutSections.title: How I Position Myself
aboutSections.copy: I sit at the intersection of data, backend systems, analytics, and applied machine learning. That gives me the ability to understand how data is produced, validated, modeled, and turned into decision-ready outputs.

aboutSections.title: Why This Portfolio Is Different
aboutSections.copy: The work here is not limited to toy notebooks or isolated dashboards. It shows enterprise-scale backend and integration work, research-style ML experimentation, data quality thinking, and communication of results through case studies.

aboutSections.title: Best Role Fit
aboutSections.copy: I am strongest in data scientist, analytics engineer, data engineer, and applied ML roles where pipeline quality, system context, and measurable outcomes matter as much as the model itself.

currentStatus.title: Open To Work
currentStatus.copy: Actively pursuing Data Scientist, Applied ML, ML Engineer, Software Engineer, and Data Engineer opportunities.

currentStatus.title: What I Want To Build
currentStatus.copy: Production-grade data products that combine clean ingestion, trustworthy analytics, and machine learning that can stand up to real usage.

currentStatus.title: Working Style
currentStatus.copy: I am most effective in environments where I can bridge engineering and analytics, work through ambiguity, and turn complex data problems into clear delivery.

profile.headline: Data Scientist | Applied ML, NLP & Deep Learning | Scalable ML & data systems on large datasets | Python | SQL | PyTorch | Spark
about: I am a data scientist with a developer's mindset and about 3 years of experience building cloud-native and data-intensive systems. My strongest work sits where messy datasets, production workflows, and machine learning meet, so I focus on turning raw information into dependable, decision-ready outputs for real operating environments.

Before and during my master's at the University of Houston-Clear Lake, I worked across Azure, .NET, SQL, Node.js, Docker, KQL, Splunk, and enterprise integration systems at TCS and Hexagon. That background shaped how I approach analytics and ML today: with equal attention to data quality, delivery reliability, system design, and measurable outcomes.

My recent work includes adversarial medical-imaging research accepted for publication and oral presentation at ICICT 2026, graph learning for disease clustering, Houston crime classification on 3.6M+ records, multimodal book-genre modeling, big-data hunger analytics, a real-time commerce platform, and an LLM-powered LinkedIn outreach agent. I am currently exploring Data Scientist, Applied ML, ML Engineer, and Data Engineering roles where I can own the path from ingestion and validation through modeling and production-minded delivery.

highlights: Mentored over 60 students in Databases, Big Data, and Python through labs and office hours.
Used a concept-first teaching approach to strengthen understanding rather than memorization.
Developed a structured feedback loop that improved student confidence and performance in programming assignments.

Architected high-availability integration landscapes using Dell Boomi to synchronize mission-critical data across Salesforce CRM, NetSuite ERP, and on-prem databases for global inventory and payroll domains.
Engineered real-time event-driven APIs and MDM flows with listeners and API gateways, applying reliability patterns to sustain 99.9% uptime for high-traffic business workflows.
Implemented AppDynamics and Splunk observability plus a custom traceability layer for migrations, reducing production incident triage time by 40%.
Led end-to-end integration delivery, translating ambiguous business requirements into reliable technical assets through architectural reviews and gap analysis.
Collaborated across distributed teams to validate end-to-end integrations and preserve data consistency and security during enterprise rollouts.
Engineered multi-partner ingestion workflows with Azure Data Factory and Durable Functions for 10+ global airline partners.
Developed high-performance gRPC and REST microservices in .NET Core and Dapper using clean architecture patterns for scalability and maintainability.
Architected SQL analytical layers for self-service bag drop systems, achieving 99.9% data accuracy and reducing manual audit overhead by 35%.
Built real-time telemetry pipelines for Tableau dashboards and deployed Application Insights plus KQL observability to maintain 24/7 operational reliability.
Optimized SQL Server, PostgreSQL, and Cosmos DB workloads and streamlined Docker plus Azure DevOps CI/CD to improve release quality for mission-critical services.

education.degree: Master of Science in Data Science
Bachelor of Technology in Mechanical Engineering

certifications: Programming for Everybody (Getting Started with Python)
Autodesk Certified Professional: AutoCAD for Design and Drafting Exam Prep

projects.title: Realtime Commerce Platform
projects.summary: Docker-first marketplace platform that combines customer and admin apps, service separation, event-driven workflows, search, recommendations, and fulfillment into a realistic end-to-end commerce system.
highlights: Expanded an earlier Node.js commerce project into a multi-service platform with gateway, GraphQL BFF, recommendation service, fulfillment service, worker, and operational admin surface.
Built separate customer and admin web applications with live cart updates, search and filtering, order tracking, warehouse-linked inventory flows, and profile management.
Modeled a more realistic commerce architecture using MySQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Redpanda, and Meilisearch to support asynchronous fulfillment, caching, event flows, and product discovery.

github.description: Docker-first real-time commerce platform with Node.js, Next.js, Redis, event streaming, GraphQL, .NET fulfillment, and recommendations.

projects.title: LinkedIn Outreach Agent
projects.summary: Chrome extension that drafts natural LinkedIn replies, recruiter responses, and connection outreach using page context, browser automation, and local or cloud LLMs.
highlights: Built a LinkedIn-native assistant that reads visible page context and drafts recruiter replies, follow-ups, comments, and connection requests in a grounded voice.
Supported multiple model backends including OpenAI, Ollama, and OpenAI-compatible local servers so the extension can run in either cloud or privacy-first local mode.
Added a review-first workflow and browser-agent capabilities so repetitive LinkedIn actions can be accelerated without losing control over the final message.

github.description: Natural LinkedIn replies, recruiter responses, and connection outreach with browser automation and local or cloud LLMs

projects.title: Adversarial Attacks on Deep Neural Networks
projects.summary: Medical imaging robustness benchmark evaluating adversarial attacks and defenses across four CNN architectures on the MURA X-ray dataset.
highlights: Benchmarked four CNN architectures on 40,561 MURA X-ray images across multiple perturbation levels using FGSM, PGD, and Carlini-Wagner attacks.
Built a preprocessing workflow with resizing, normalization, CLAHE enhancement, bone windowing, and smart cropping for clinically consistent inputs.
Implemented FGSM adversarial training with 50% clean and 50% adversarial batches over 50 epochs, improving FGSM robustness and yielding moderate PGD gains.

github.description: This project investigates both adversarial attacks and defensive strategies for Deep Neural Networks (DNNs). While adversarial examples exploit vulnerabilities in models by introducing imperceptible perturbations, defensive methods aim to improve robustness and ensure reliable predictions.

projects.title: Houston Crime Type Classification (2010-2024)
projects.summary: Large-scale crime analytics pipeline on roughly 3.6M Houston crime records to predict NIBRS offense categories under a realistic temporal holdout design.
highlights: Standardized and merged three differently formatted source datasets into one modeling-ready schema spanning 2010 to 2024.
Engineered Beat, Premise, Month, Weekday, and Hour Group features and cleaned multi-label crime records into separate observations.
Selected MLPClassifier as the best model, reaching 29.37% accuracy and 0.5895 macro AUC on the 2024 holdout set.

github.description: An end-to-end machine learning pipeline built to predict crime categories (NIBRS Description) using 14 years of Houston Police Department data (~3.6 million records), with 2024 used as a true future holdout year.

projects.title: Disease Networks for Disease Clustering
projects.summary: End-to-end graph learning pipeline that combines disease-gene networks with Gene Ontology features to improve unsupervised biomedical disease clustering.
highlights: Built disease x gene and disease x GO feature matrices to combine graph topology with biological process semantics.
Trained a 3-layer GCN encoder and clustered learned latent embeddings with KMeans for stronger disease-group separation.
Improved inter-vs-intra cluster distance separation from 0.90 to 1.45, 0.85 to 1.25, and 1.10 to 1.85 across 40, 50, and 60-disease subsets.

projects.title: CivicQuest
projects.summary: AI-supported community engagement platform for volunteering discovery, sustainability learning, and guided interaction.
highlights: AI-powered community engagement platform
Volunteer discovery and registration journeys
Gamified sustainability learning plus Azure bot integration

github.description: AI-powered community engagement platform that helps users discover local volunteering events, register seamlessly, learn sustainability through a gamified experience, and interact with an Azure-based chatbot — built with Node.js, Express, SQLite, and Azure services.

projects.title: Multimodal Book Genre Classification and Clustering
projects.summary: Multimodal content-intelligence pipeline that combines book titles and cover images to classify and segment books across large catalog datasets.
highlights: Built a dual-modality workflow using Word2Vec and LSTM for titles plus CNN-based visual features for covers across Book32 and BookCover30.
Performed large-scale preprocessing and missing-data analysis before classification and clustering.
Validated genre separation with KMeans and PCA, surfacing interpretable clusters such as Self-Help, Religion and Spirituality, and Education.

github.description: A scalable multimodal machine learning pipeline for book genre prediction, clustering, and segmentation using both book cover images and textual metadata (titles).  This project combines Computer Vision + NLP to automatically organize and segment large-scale book catalogs for recommendation systems and digital libraries.

projects.title: Zero Hunger Big Data Analytics Pipeline
projects.summary: Big-data pipeline aligned to UN SDG 2 that processes 86,657 records across hunger, agriculture, and socio-economic indicators into scalable analytics and forecasting workflows.
highlights: Designed ingestion, transformation, and querying workflows across HDFS, Hadoop Streaming MapReduce, Spark, and Hive.
Normalized public data sources including FAO, World Bank, and UNICEF-style datasets into analysis-ready tables for hunger hotspot detection.
Built forecasting and visualization workflows, with project materials reporting about 95% prediction accuracy for hunger-trend evaluation.

projects.title: ASP.NET Core Docs (Fork)
projects.summary: Reference fork of ASP.NET Core documentation used for study and internal notes.
highlights: Maintained as a working reference repo
Useful for .NET architecture and API patterns
Supports learning and implementation review

projects.title: Boomi CI/CD Experiments
projects.summary: Enterprise integration and reliability work around Boomi-based distributed workflows, runtime observability, and support automation.
highlights: Supported 10M+ daily transactions across distributed runtimes while sustaining 99.9% SLA uptime for business-critical workflows.
Implemented validation and control checks across 6 ingestion pipelines to reduce downstream failures and improve data consistency.
Automated health checks, restart workflows, and runbooks to eliminate recurring failures and reduce onboarding time for new engineers.

github.description: For testing out the Boomi CI/CD

projects.title: deploy.github.io
projects.summary: Minimal static deployment experiment for lightweight hosting and GitHub Pages-style publication.
highlights: Lightweight static deployment setup
Useful baseline for simple hosting workflows
Small experiment in publishing and delivery

projects.title: Microservices Using .NET Core
projects.summary: Clean-architecture microservices system using messaging, RPC, caching, and multiple persistence layers.
highlights: Designed loosely coupled services with clean architecture
Used RabbitMQ and gRPC for inter-service communication
Applied cache and polyglot persistence patterns

github.description: Developing Loosely Coupled Application via netcore using services mongodb,redis as a Database or Cache,Postgre Sql, Sql using EntityFrameworkCore Implementing Clean Architecture with RabbitMQ,GRPC, and Ocelot Gateway

projects.title: Node E-Commerce Use Case
projects.summary: API-oriented e-commerce use case covering shopping flows, backend logic, and service design basics.
highlights: Implemented core e-commerce backend flows
Used API-first design for shopping interactions
Built a foundation for larger full-stack demos
